creator: denis5594@gmail.com
text:
Newer ALPS touchpads require special sequence to turn into "touchpad"
mode, according to new protocol they using. Unfortunately, this specification
is proprietary, and not well-known. However, there is a patch that can
make touchpad act like ImPS/2 intellimouse, thus allowing to use horizontal
scrolling and right click. Bad news - its a patch for linux kernel, not
freebsd. I've tried to figure out how psmouse from linux and psm working,
and it seems like this patch can be sucsessfully ported to freebsd.

Fix: This patch gives at least ability to scroll or right-click

Patch attached with submission follows:
How-To-Repeat: Any new ALPS Glidepoint touchpad will work that way
